g.data-narration: Welcome to Circular Queues! Today we'll explore how this clever data structure solves the memory waste problem of linear queues.
g.text: Understanding Efficient Queue Implementation with Modular Arithmetic

g.data-narration: The Problem: In a linear queue, when we dequeue elements, the freed space at the front becomes wasted. New elements can only be added at the rear.

text.tspan: Linear queues waste memory: After dequeuing,
front space remains unused even when rear reaches end.

g.data-narration: The Solution: Circular Queue connects the rear to the front logically. When rear reaches the end, it wraps around to reuse freed space at the front.

text.tspan: Circular queue treats array as a ring. When rear reaches end,
it wraps to index 0 if space is available (FIFO + reuse).

g.data-narration: Key Terminology: Front points to the first element (to be dequeued), Rear points to the last element (where new elements are added).
The Magic Formula: Index modulo capacity creates the wrap-around effect. When index reaches N, modulo returns to 0.
Critical Conditions: isEmpty returns true when front equals -1 (initial state). isFull uses the one-slot-empty strategy: rear+1 equals front.
g.text: One slot stays empty to distinguish full from empty

g.data-narration: Enqueue Operation: First check if full. For first element, set front=rear=0. Otherwise, increment rear using modulo, then insert value.
Dequeue Operation: First check if empty. Store the front element, then if only one element, reset queue. Otherwise, increment front using modulo.
Let us trace through operations on a queue of capacity 5. Initial state: front=-1, rear=-1, all slots empty.
After enqueueing 10, 20, 30, 40: front=0, rear=3. Elements fill indices 0-3. Index 4 remains empty (one slot strategy).
After dequeuing twice: 10 and 20 are removed. front moves to 2. The freed space at indices 0-1 can be reused. rear stays at 3.
Now comes the magic. Enqueue 50 goes to rear=4. Enqueue 60 wraps around: rear=(4+1)%5=0. Queue reuses freed space!
The queue is now full. With isFull condition: (0+1)%5 = 1 equals front. Note: index 1 is empty but queue considers itself full.
Comparison: Linear queue wastes space after repeated enqueue/dequeue. Circular queue reuses freed space, making it memory efficient.
Applications: CPU scheduling (Round Robin), buffering in OS, network packets, producer-consumer problem. Key: O(1) ops with efficient memory.
g.text: Key: All operations O(1), wraps using modulo, one slot empty strategy
